opportunities.title: 622 and Counting: Inside the Worst Patch Tuesday Ever Recorded — What Actually Needs Your Attention This Week
opportunities.why_now: Microsoft just shattered its own record from last month with 622 CVEs, and two are already under active exploitation, making triage the real story.
opportunities.angle: Digest/breakdown format: rank the handful of CVEs that matter (the two zero-days) against the noise of 620 others, with a patch-priority cheat sheet

opportunities.title: SonicWall's SMA 1000 Has Two Zero-Days in the Wild — Patch Before You Read the Rest of This List
opportunities.why_now: SonicWall issued an urgent warning for two actively exploited zero-days on its SMA 1000 line, one of which grants admin-level commands to attackers.
opportunities.angle: Breaking/urgent advisory style post: what's exploited, who's exposed, and the patch timeline — the classic 'stop scrolling and patch this now' post

opportunities.title: Your AI Coding Assistant Will Run Malware For You: Cursor IDE Auto-Executes Code From Poisoned Repos
opportunities.why_now: Cursor's flaw lets a simple git clone trigger Windows code execution, landing right as developers rush to adopt AI-assisted IDEs without threat-modeling them.
opportunities.angle: Explainer with attack-chain walkthrough: how a booby-trapped repo becomes RCE, and why 'AI IDE' security review is now a checklist item

opportunities.title: Progress Pulled the ShareFile Plug on Purpose: The Zero-Day Behind the Mystery Shutdown, Explained
opportunities.why_now: What began as a vague 'mystery security threat' emergency shutdown has now been confirmed as an actual zero-day, closing the loop on a story that broke across multiple outlets this week.
opportunities.angle: Timeline/follow-up piece: from cryptic shutdown notice to confirmed CVE, with lessons on vendor communication during active incidents

opportunities.title: The Kremlin Did It: EU and UK Officially Pin Poland's Power Grid Hack on Russian Spies
opportunities.why_now: This is a rare formal state attribution for a critical-infrastructure attack, escalating the stakes for European energy security amid ongoing Russian cyber operations.
opportunities.angle: Geopolitical breakdown: what the attribution actually proves, what it doesn't, and what it means for grid operators watching Russian TTPs
